zh.lifeSummary: 當Anne Barne於1587年2月2日出生在英國英格蘭米德萊可哈可尼時，她的父親Sir William Barne of Woolwich18歲，母親Anne Sandys17歲。她於大約1610年與Sir William Lovelace V在英國結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1633年5月22日之前在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 安德魯 白 馮 瓦德洛貝 煙雨 聖 安 布拉可福利亞斯，埋葬在英國肯特坎特布瑞。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anne Barne于1587年2月2日出生在英国英格兰米德萊可哈可尼时，她的父亲Sir William Barne of Woolwich18岁，母亲Anne Sandys17岁。她于大约1610年与Sir William Lovelace V在英国结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1633年5月22日之前在英国英格兰倫敦聖 安德魯 白 馮 瓦德洛貝 煙雨 聖 安 布拉可福利亞斯，埋葬在英国肯特坎特布瑞。

nameMeanings.name: Barne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Barn
meanings: Scottish:  see  Barne   and  compare  Berne  .

variants.name: Barner
meanings: English: in southern England a habitational name for someone who lived by a barn, from Middle English berner ‘dweller by the barn’. Elsewhere possibly a variant of Berner .
Scottish: unexplained.
North German: derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Barnher or Bernher (see Berner 6).

variants.name: Barnes
meanings: English: habitational name from Barnes (on the Surrey bank of the Thames in London), named with Old English bere-ærn ‘barn, a storehouse for barley and other grain’, or a topographic name or metonymic occupational name for someone who lived by or worked at a barn or barns, from Middle English barn ‘barn, granary’.

English: variant of Barne, with excrescent -s, derived from either the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn (based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn or Old English Beorn, both from a word meaning ‘warrior’), or from Middle English barn (Old Norse barn) ‘child’. The latter term is found as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child (see Child ).
Irish: in Ireland in many cases this is no doubt the English name, but in others it is possibly an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Bearáin ‘descendant of Bearán’, a byname meaning ‘spear’.

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Bern, Barn. In the Midlands and East Anglia this is based on the Scandinavian personal name Biǫrn, anglicized as Beorn; in the south-west it is from Old English Beorn; both names are from a word meaning ‘warrior’. Some of the following surname bearers may alternatively belong with other senses below.

2 English: status name from Middle English barn (Old Scandinavian barn) ‘child’. This term is found in Domesday Book as a byname for men of the upper classes; it might also have had the meaning ‘young man of a prominent family’, like Middle English child ( see Child ).

3 English: locative name, nickname for residence near or employment at a barn, Middle English bern (Old English bere-ærn). See also Barnes . Some of the post-medieval bearers may alternatively belong with (1) or (2).

variants.name: Berne
meanings: French: from a short form of the personal name Bernard or any other name containing the ancient Germanic element ber(n) ‘bear’.
Altered form of Swiss German   Berni  .
English and Scottish: variant of Barne (see Barnes 2) or Burn .

variants.name: Bearne
meanings: Scots: Bearn, a child, a human creature

variants.name: Byrne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Aidan, Liam, Niall, Declan, Eamonn, Fergus, John Patrick, Keelin, Kieran, Siobhan.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Broin ‘descendant of Bran’, a personal name based on bran ‘raven’. Bran was the name of a son of the King of Leinster, who died at Cologne in 1052, and also of the hero of an 8th century voyage tale.
Irish: in some cases, possibly from Ó Birn ‘descendant of Biorn’, Irish form of the Old Norse personal name Bjǫrn.

variants.name: Child
meanings: English:

nickname from Middle English child ‘child, infant’ (Old English cild), in various possible applications. The word is found in Old English as a byname, and in Middle English as a widely used affectionate term of address. It was also used as a term of status for a young man of noble birth, although the exact meaning is not clear; in the 13th and 14th centuries it was a technical term used of a young noble awaiting elevation to the knighthood. In other cases it may have been applied as a byname to a youth considerably younger than his brothers or to one who was a minor on the death of his father.
in Kent, possibly a topographic name from Old English cielde ‘spring (water)’, a rare word derived from c(e)ald ‘cold’.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Anne Barne was born on 2 February 1587, in Hackney, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William Barne of Woolwich, was 18 and her mother, Anne Sandys, was 17. She married Sir William Lovelace V about 1610, in England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died before 22 May 1633, in St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St Ann Blackfriars, London, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Canterbury, Kent, England.

Elle a épousé Sir William Lovelace V environ 1610, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée avant 22 mai 1633, à St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St Ann Blackfriars, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Canterbury, Kent, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne Barneが1587年2月2日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスハクニーで生まれた時，父Sir William Barne of Woolwichは18歳，母Anne Sandysは17歳であった。約1610年にイギリスでSir William Lovelace Vと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1633年5月22日以前にイギリスイギリスロンドンスト アンドリュー ビー ス ワードロベ ウィス スト アン ブラクフリアルズで死去し，イギリスケントカンテルブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.London St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 앤드루 비 테 워드로브 위스 스트 아 블랙프리아르스
ko.lifeSummary: Anne Barne(이)가 1587년 2월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 해c니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Barne of Woolwich(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Sandys(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1610년 영국에서 Sir William Lovelace V(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1633년 5월 22일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 앤드루 비 테 워드로브 위스 스트 아 블랙프리아르스에서 사망했으며 영국 켄트 캉르버이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anne Barne nasceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1587, em Hackney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William Barne of Woolwich, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Anne Sandys, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Lovelace V aproximadamente 1610, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 22 de maio de 1633, em St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St Ann Blackfriars, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anne Barne родилась 2 февраля 1587 г. в местности хаккни, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William Barne of Woolwich было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Sandys, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Lovelace V около 1610 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла перед 22 май 1633 г. в местности ст андрю би т вардроб вит ст анн блэккфрярс, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности кантирбури, кэнт, Англия.

es.02 Feb 1587–before 22 May 1633: 2 de febrero de 1587–antes de 22 de mayo de 1633
es.London St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St: St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St Ann Blackfriars, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anne Barne nació el 2 de febrero de 1587, en Hackney, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William Barne of Woolwich, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Anne Sandys, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir William Lovelace V aproximadamente 1610, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió antes de 22 de mayo de 1633, en St Andrew by the Wardrobe with St Ann Blackfriars, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Canterbury, Kent, Inglaterra.
